questions.q: Who led the Dandi Salt March in 1930?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the Dandi Salt March in 1930.

questions.q: From which ashram did the Salt March begin?
questions.explanation: The march began from Sabarmati Ashram near Ahmedabad.

questions.q: What law did Gandhi break at the end of the march?
questions.explanation: Gandhi broke the salt law by making salt from seawater at Dandi.

questions.q: To which coastal village did Gandhi march to make salt?
questions.explanation: Gandhi marched to the coastal village of Dandi in Gujarat.

questions.q: Approximately how long was the route of the Dandi March?
questions.explanation: The march covered roughly 240 miles (about 385 km) from Sabarmati to Dandi.

questions.q: The Salt March launched which larger nationwide campaign?
questions.explanation: The Salt March launched the Civil Disobedience Movement.

questions.q: In which year did the Dandi Salt March take place?
questions.explanation: The Salt March took place in March and April 1930.

questions.q: What everyday item was at the centre of the Salt March protest?
questions.explanation: The march protested the British monopoly and tax on salt, a daily necessity.

questions.q: The Dandi March is also commonly known by what other name?
questions.explanation: The march is also called the Salt Satyagraha.

questions.q: Dandi is located in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Dandi is a coastal village in the state of Gujarat.

questions.q: On which date did Gandhi begin the Salt March from Sabarmati?
questions.explanation: Gandhi set out from Sabarmati on 12 March 1930.

questions.q: On which date did Gandhi reach Dandi and break the salt law?
questions.explanation: Gandhi reached Dandi and ceremonially made salt on 6 April 1930.

questions.q: Roughly how many days did the Salt March from Sabarmati to Dandi take?
questions.explanation: The march lasted about 24 days, from 12 March to 6 April 1930.

questions.q: How many volunteers initially set out with Gandhi from Sabarmati Ashram?
questions.explanation: Gandhi began the march with around 78 chosen ashram volunteers, with thousands joining along the way.

questions.q: Before starting, Gandhi sent a letter announcing his intentions to which official?
questions.explanation: Gandhi wrote to Viceroy Lord Irwin announcing his plan to break the salt law.

questions.q: The Salt March drew worldwide attention partly because of coverage by what?
questions.explanation: International journalists followed the march, giving it global publicity.

questions.q: Which famous woman poet-leader later led the Dharasana salt raid that followed the Dandi March?
questions.explanation: Sarojini Naidu led the nonviolent raid on the Dharasana Salt Works after the Dandi March.

questions.q: What did Gandhi do at Dandi to symbolically break the law?
options: Picked up natural salt from the shore
questions.explanation: Gandhi picked up a lump of natural salt left by the sea, breaking the salt monopoly.

questions.q: The salt tax was seen as especially unjust because it burdened whom most?
questions.explanation: Salt was a daily necessity, so the tax fell hardest on the poorest sections of society.

questions.q: The Salt March received the sanction of which Congress declaration made shortly before it?
questions.explanation: The 1929 Lahore session's Purna Swaraj resolution authorised civil disobedience, which began with the Salt March.

questions.q: In southern India, a parallel salt march was led at Vedaranyam by which leader?
questions.explanation: C. Rajagopalachari led a salt satyagraha march to Vedaranyam in the south, paralleling Gandhi's march.

questions.q: Through how many villages did the Dandi March pass on its way to the coast?
questions.explanation: The march passed through dozens of villages, where Gandhi addressed crowds and recruited support along the route.

questions.q: The Dandi March began from Sabarmati Ashram, which Gandhi had founded near Ahmedabad in which year?
questions.explanation: Sabarmati Ashram was established by Gandhi in 1917, and the 1930 march set out from there.

questions.q: After Gandhi reached Dandi, he was arrested by the British in early May 1930 while planning a raid on which salt works?
questions.explanation: Gandhi was arrested before his planned raid on the Dharasana Salt Works; the raid then went ahead under other leaders.

questions.q: The Dandi March route ran roughly through which district region of Gujarat toward the coast?
questions.explanation: The march wound through the Kheda and Surat regions of Gujarat to reach the coast at Dandi.

questions.q: Gandhi vowed before the march that he would not return to Sabarmati Ashram until what was achieved?
options: He was elected to the legislature
questions.explanation: Gandhi pledged not to return to Sabarmati Ashram until India had won swaraj, and he never did.

questions.q: Which British-era law established the salt monopoly that the march defied, dating back largely to which period?
options: Nineteenth-century salt acts under colonial rule
questions.explanation: British salt laws and the salt monopoly dated from nineteenth-century colonial legislation, which the march challenged.

questions.q: The Dharasana Salt Works raid that followed was vividly reported by which American journalist?
questions.explanation: Webb Miller's eyewitness report of the brutal Dharasana raid spread news of the satyagraha worldwide.

questions.q: The Civil Disobedience Movement that the Salt March launched was eventually suspended by which 1931 agreement?
questions.explanation: The Gandhi-Irwin Pact of March 1931 suspended the Civil Disobedience Movement and made concessions on salt.

questions.q: To commemorate the march, a national memorial was opened at Dandi in which decade?
questions.explanation: The National Salt Satyagraha Memorial at Dandi was inaugurated in 2019, commemorating the march.
